channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: ICE shared Medicaid data it wasn't supposed to have with Palantir
item.description: The revelations came out in a federal court case brought by Democratic states challenging ICE's access to Medicaid data to aid in deportation efforts.
item.content:encoded:
The revelations came out in a federal court case brought by Democratic states challenging ICE's access to Medicaid data to aid in deportation efforts.

(Image credit: Adam Gray)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Ex-wife says ICE agent who killed man in Maine had racist beliefs, violent tendencies
item.description: Ashley Brouillette has identified her ex-husband, David Brouillette, as the officer who fatally shot Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday. She said she learned he was the officer responsible when he called her on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
Ashley Brouillette has identified her ex-husband, David Brouillette, as the officer who fatally shot Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday. She said she learned he was the officer responsible when he called her on Wednesday.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: The new $1 Trump coin doesn't just buck norms. Experts say it also breaks laws
item.description: President Trump's face will appear on a new commemorative coin honoring the nation's 250th birthday. It's one of the many unusual places his likeness has popped up this year.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's face will appear on a new commemorative coin honoring the nation's 250th birthday. It's one of the many unusual places his likeness has popped up this year.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Wildfire smoke is like smoking 'half a pack a day.' Here's how to protect yourself
item.description: As Canadian wildfires spread smoke across the U.S. the air pollution is dangerous to health. But there are ways to protect yourself. Here's what to know.
item.content:encoded:
As Canadian wildfires spread smoke across the U.S. the air pollution is dangerous to health. But there are ways to protect yourself. Here's what to know.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda

item.title: Is smoke in your home? Here's how to make an air purifier using a box fan
item.description: Smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is sending air quality indexes to record levels, posing a hazard for millions of people. Here are tips for how to breathe easier in your home.
item.content:encoded:
Smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is sending air quality indexes to record levels, posing a hazard for millions of people. Here are tips for how to breathe easier in your home.

(Image credit: Finn Gomez)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Photos: When the World Cup came to town
item.description: NPR member station photographers captured images of World Cup watch parties outside stadium gates, on street corners and in public parks and squares as the World Cup became, for a brief period, a part of local life.
item.content:encoded:
NPR member station photographers captured images of World Cup watch parties outside stadium gates, on street corners and in public parks and squares as the World Cup became, for a brief period, a part of local life.

(Image credit: Patricia Lim)
item.dc:creator: Meredith Nierman

item.title: Wildfire smoke continues to spread across parts of the U.S.
item.description: Dozens of U.S. cities are dealing with poor air quality, as wildfires smoke turns skies hazy and orange. Conditions won't improve until the weekend, prompting officials to urge people to stay indoors.
item.content:encoded: Dozens of U.S. cities are dealing with poor air quality, as wildfires smoke turns skies hazy and orange. Conditions won't improve until the weekend, prompting officials to urge people to stay indoors.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: What will the Pentagon begin screening 'war fighters' for? The quiz knows
item.description: Plus: Jurassic Park, U.K. politics, conspiracy theories, Pete Hegseth and numismatics.
item.content:encoded: Plus: Jurassic Park, U.K. politics, conspiracy theories, Pete Hegseth and numismatics.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump raises claims that U.S. voting systems are vulnerable in speech, election experts say the speech and released unclassified docs provide no evidence, dangerous floods sweep parts of Texas.
item.content:encoded: Trump raises claims that U.S. voting systems are vulnerable in speech, election experts say the speech and released unclassified docs provide no evidence, dangerous floods sweep parts of Texas.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: NPR reporter learns how to crab on the Chesapeake Bay
item.description: NPR's Frank Langfitt tries his hand at catching crabs on the Chesapeake Bay. Maryland has more than 5,000 commercial licenses, but tens of thousands more crab for fun.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Frank Langfitt tries his hand at catching crabs on the Chesapeake Bay. Maryland has more than 5,000 commercial licenses, but tens of thousands more crab for fun.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt
